events.title: BBC "Les sanglots longs" go-signal to Resistance
events.description: Second half of Verlaine's couplet broadcast at 21:15 BST signalling French Resistance to begin sabotage of rail and communications.

events.title: IX TCC Pathfinders depart England
events.description: First C-47 Pathfinder serials lift off from North Witham; their job is to mark the airborne DZs/LZs with Eureka beacons and lights.

events.title: Pegasus Bridge taken (6th Airborne)
events.description: Coup-de-main by Major Howard's D Coy / 2nd Ox & Bucks LI in 6 Horsa gliders against the Bénouville bridge over the Caen Canal. Held against counterattacks until Lovat's commandos arrived at noon.

events.title: Horsa Bridge (Ranville) taken (6th Airborne)
events.description: Companion bridge over the Orne river taken by another Howard glider stick within minutes of Pegasus.

events.title: 101st Airborne first sticks over Cotentin
events.description: 101st pathfinders go in first, then assault sticks: 502 PIR to DZ-A (Saint-Martin-de-Varreville), 506 PIR to DZ-C (Hiesville), 501 PIR to DZ-D (Vierville). Sticks scatter widely across the Cotentin interior due to weather and AAA.

events.title: 82nd Airborne first sticks over DZ-O
events.description: First 82nd sticks (505 PIR) over Sainte-Mère-Église; widely scattered due to weather and AAA, much like the 101st.

events.title: Generalleutnant Falley killed (91. LL CO)
events.description: 91. Luftlande-Division commander killed near Picauville while returning from a Kriegsspiel in Rennes; ambushed by a 508 PIR patrol. Decapitates German Cotentin command early.

disputedBy.claim: Sources disagree on the exact time (anywhere from c. 03:00 to 06:00 local), location, and identifying patrol; Harrison, Zetterling, and Beevor differ. The broad facts (CO killed in transit near Picauville) are not disputed.

events.title: Sainte-Mère-Église taken (3/505 PIR)
events.description: First French town liberated. LtCol Krause hoists the same flag the 505 carried in Naples earlier in the war.

events.title: Merville Battery silenced (9 Para)
events.description: Lt-Col Otway's 9 Para assault on the Merville coastal battery. Mission accomplished but at very heavy cost; battery proved to be 75mm not the 150mm Allied intel feared.

events.title: Naval bombardment opens
events.description: Battleships and cruisers of TF 124 (Omaha) and TF 125 (Utah) open fire on coastal batteries and identified strongpoints.

events.title: HMS Belfast ouvre le feu sur La Marefontaine
events.description: À 05:30, HMS Belfast (flag Force E) ouvre la première salve d'obus de 6 pouces sur la batterie de La Marefontaine (Ver-sur-Mer). Première lourde salve de la flotte alliée le jour J.

events.title: HMS Warspite vs batterie de Villerville
events.description: Le cuirassé HMS Warspite (15 pouces) engage la batterie de Villerville à l'est de l'Orne, neutralisant un risque de tir d'enfilade sur Sword.

events.title: HMS Ramillies dissuade Le Havre
events.description: HMS Ramillies tire sur les batteries de Bénerville et Houlgate, et reste en posture de blocage face à toute sortie potentielle de destroyers ou de S-Boote depuis Le Havre.

events.title: USS Texas tire sur Pointe-du-Hoc
events.description: À 05:50 le cuirassé USS Texas (14 pouces) ouvre le feu sur la position fortifiée de Pointe-du-Hoc, préparant l'assaut des Rangers de Rudder qui débute à 07:10.

events.title: USS Nevada vs batterie de Crisbecq
events.description: USS Nevada — survivante de Pearl Harbor — engage la batterie de Crisbecq (Saint-Marcouf, 4×210 mm) en concert avec HMS Erebus. La batterie tirera encore le 12 juin, jamais entièrement réduite par le feu naval.

events.title: USS Arkansas + HMS Glasgow ouvrent sur Omaha central
events.description: USS Arkansas (12 pouces) et HMS Glasgow (6 pouces) ouvrent le feu sur les strongpoints WN-65/WN-66 et la zone Les Moulins / Easy Red. La densité du feu reste insuffisante face aux bunkers en béton du 916. IR.

events.title: Montcalm + Georges Leygues vs batterie de Longues-sur-Mer
events.description: Les croiseurs des Forces Navales Françaises Libres FNS Montcalm et FNS Georges Leygues ouvrent le feu sur la batterie de Longues-sur-Mer (4×152 mm), première action navale française majeure en eaux françaises depuis 1940.

events.title: Duel HMS Ajax vs Longues-sur-Mer
events.description: HMS Ajax (vétéran du combat de la Plata) duelle avec la batterie de Longues à courte portée et la fait taire vers 08:45. La batterie est capturée intacte par le 50th Northumbrian le 7 juin (post-MVP).

events.title: USS Augusta : Bradley redirige les vagues Omaha
events.description: Depuis le pont du croiseur lourd USS Augusta, le LtGen Bradley reçoit un flot de rapports alarmants sur Omaha (« la situation est désespérée ») et envisage de rediriger les vagues suivantes vers Utah ou Gold. La tendance se renverse vers 11:00 quand la première brèche du bluff arrive.

events.title: USS Texas s'approche pour Vierville
events.description: Avec les exits Omaha encore bouchés, USS Texas se met en position rapprochée (environ 7 brasses sous la quille) pour délivrer un tir direct sur les défenses du draw de Vierville (D-1). Risque opérationnel assumé pour appuyer le 116th RCT.

events.title: Heavy bomber strike on Omaha overshoots
events.description: 8th AF B-24s release late through cloud to avoid hitting incoming landing craft; bombs fall up to several kilometers inland, missing the bluff defenses.

disputedBy.claim: Sources differ on the exact scale and consequence of the overshoot — Harrison's Green Books note bombs fell '1–3 miles inland'; later analysis (cited in Beevor) suggests some bomb groups did hit closer to the bluff line in places.

events.title: H-Hour Omaha — first wave touchdown
events.description: Lead waves of 16th RCT (1st ID) at Easy Red/Fox Green and 116th RCT (29th ID) at Dog Green/Dog White touch down. Heavy fire from intact bluff defenses (916 IR / 352. ID).

events.title: H-Hour Utah — first wave touchdown
events.description: Lead 8th IR / 4th ID landings; current pushed waves ~2km south of planned aim point — Roosevelt Jr.'s "we'll start the war from right here".

disputedBy.claim: Sources give offsets between 1,800 and 2,000 yards south of the planned Tare Green / Uncle Red aim point; the southerly drift turned out to be operationally fortunate (lighter defenses).

events.title: 21. Panzer release order (Feuchtinger)
events.description: Authorisation to commit 21. Panzer west of the Orne reaches Feuchtinger. The exact timing is one of the most-disputed staff-record questions of D-Day.

disputedBy.claim: Allied accounts have placed the formal release as late as ~14:00 ; German staff records show authorisation by mid-morning. Feuchtinger had also already engaged elements east of the Orne against 6th Airborne overnight on local initiative.

events.title: Rangers scale Pointe-du-Hoc
events.description: 2nd Ranger Bn (LtCol Rudder) scale the cliffs under fire; objective battery found dummy-emplaced, real guns located inland and destroyed.

events.title: H-Hour Gold (50th Northumbrian)
events.description: British 50th Division lands at Gold Beach (Le Hamel / La Rivière). 231 Brigade on Jig sectors, 69 Brigade on King.

events.title: H-Hour Sword (3rd British)
events.description: British 3rd Division lands at Sword Beach (Queen White / Queen Red). 8 Brigade lead, with 1st Special Service Brigade (Lovat) and Free French Cdo Kieffer attached for the Ouistreham casino.

events.title: H-Hour Juno (3rd Canadian)
events.description: 3rd Canadian Division lands at Juno Beach (Mike on Courseulles, Nan on Bernières / Saint-Aubin). Touchdown 10 min late due to tide on the offshore reef.

events.title: Le Hamel strongpoint (WN37) resists
events.description: 1st Hampshires take heavy losses against WN37 (Le Hamel) overlooking Jig Green ; the strongpoint holds out into the afternoon and is reduced piecemeal by AVRE Petards.

events.title: Saint-Marie-du-Mont taken (1/506 PIR)
events.description: Sink's 506 PIR rallies and clears the village — key inland anchor behind Utah causeways.

events.title: Omaha first wave pinned at the seawall
events.description: By 08:00 most of 16th and 116th RCT lead companies are pinned along the seawall and shingle, taking artillery and MG-42 fire from the bluffs. Beach exits unopened.

events.title: Ouistreham casino taken (Cdo Kieffer)
events.description: Free French Cdo Kieffer (177 men, attached to 1st Special Service Brigade) take the heavily fortified Ouistreham casino strongpoint at the Orne mouth. The first major French combat action on home soil since 1940.

events.title: La Rivière cleared (5 East Yorks)
events.description: 5 East Yorkshires clear La Rivière (Ver-sur-Mer) on the King Red side of Gold ; opens the eastern Gold exits inland.

events.title: Courseulles-sur-Mer cleared (Reg. de la Chaudière + Royal Winnipeg)
events.description: Mike sectors of Juno cleared : Royal Winnipeg Rifles + Regina Rifles (7 Brigade) take Courseulles-sur-Mer after heavy close-quarter fighting in the streets.

events.title: First engineer beach exits blown
events.description: Combat engineers (5th and 6th ESB) blow gaps at E-1 (Saint-Laurent draw) and partial gap at E-3 (Colleville draw), enabling armor and follow-up waves to pass.

events.title: Asnelles-sur-Mer cleared (1 Hampshires)
events.description: 1st Hampshires (231 Brigade) clear Asnelles-sur-Mer after the WN37 fall. Opens the central Jig sector inland.

events.title: Bernières-sur-Mer cleared (Queen's Own Rifles)
events.description: Bernières-sur-Mer cleared by Queen's Own Rifles of Canada after taking the heaviest single-company casualties of any Allied first wave.

disputedBy.claim: Casualty figures for B Coy QOR on Nan White vary across QOR, brigade, and divisional war diaries from ~61 to ~95 men down within minutes ; 'highest single-company casualties of any Allied first wave on D-Day' is widely cited but the exact comparison is contested.

events.title: Hermanville-sur-Mer taken (2 East Yorks)
events.description: 2 East Yorkshires (8 Brigade) clear Hermanville-sur-Mer south of Sword's Queen White. First inland anchor on the Sword axis.

events.title: First bluff-line breach behind Easy Red
events.description: Small-unit infiltration up the bluffs in the gap between strongpoints WN-62 and WN-64 — the first sustained Allied foothold on the Omaha plateau.

events.title: Saint-Aubin-sur-Mer cleared (North Shore Regt)
events.description: North Shore Regiment (8 Brigade) clears Saint-Aubin-sur-Mer ; eastern hardpoint of Juno. Opens link toward Sword.

events.title: 4th ID — 101st link-up at Causeway 2
events.description: Lead 4th ID elements moving inland from Utah meet 1/506 PIR holding Causeway 2 (Houdienville–Pouppeville). Utah lodgement now joined to the airborne pocket.

events.title: 21. Panzer transits Caen under air interdiction
events.description: 21. Panzer's main body crawls north through Caen toward the Sword/Juno gap. Allied fighter-bombers (Typhoons of 83 Group) interdict the column ; movement is hours behind schedule.

events.title: Reviers passed (1st Hussars + Regina Rifles)
events.description: Lead 7 Brigade armoured group (1st Hussars Shermans) and Regina Rifles push through Reviers, opening the road south of Juno.

events.title: Vierville draw (D-1) secured
events.description: 29th ID elements + Rangers clear the Vierville draw, opening the western Omaha exit for armor and follow-up.

events.title: Lord Lovat's commandos relieve Pegasus Bridge
events.description: 1st Special Service Brigade marches inland from Sword Beach (piped in by Bill Millin) and links up with Howard's force at Pegasus Bridge.

events.title: Battle for La Fière causeway
events.description: 82nd Airborne (505 PIR + 507 PIR elements) contests the La Fière bridge over the Merderet against 1057 GR (91. LL). Causeway changes hands repeatedly.

events.title: Bény-sur-Mer passed (8 Brigade)
events.description: 8 Canadian Brigade pushes through Bény-sur-Mer toward Anguerny / Anisy ; deepest inland push of any Allied division on D-Day.

events.title: 716. ID combat-broken
events.description: By mid-afternoon the German 716. Infanterie-Division has lost cohesion across all three British/Canadian beach sectors ; surviving battalions falling back through the Périers ridge or absorbed by 21. Panzer.

events.title: Bieville: Sherman Fireflies vs Pz-Rgt 22
events.description: Staffordshire Yeomanry Sherman Fireflies dug in north of Bieville knock out ~13 Pz IV from 21. Panzer's Pz-Rgt 22 in the first major tank engagement of the campaign. The 21. Pz drive on the coast is blunted.

events.title: Saint-Laurent-sur-Mer entered (1st & 29th ID)
events.description: Mixed 1st and 29th ID elements work into Saint-Laurent-sur-Mer from the bluff line; village contested through the afternoon.

events.title: Anguerny / Anisy reached (1st Hussars recce)
events.description: 1st Hussars and Inns of Court recce patrols reach Anguerny and Anisy, ~13 km from the beach — the deepest D-Day penetration of any Allied division. Patrols withdraw before nightfall.

events.title: 21. Panzer (KG Rauch) reaches the coast at Lion-sur-Mer
events.description: Kampfgruppe Rauch (Pz-Gren-Rgt 192) of 21. Panzer reaches the coast at Lion-sur-Mer in the late afternoon, briefly threatening to split Sword from Juno. Withdraws under pressure as British 9 Brigade closes the gap.

disputedBy.claim: The narrative claim that '21. Panzer reached the sea' is sometimes overstated. KG Rauch (a single battalion-strength Kampfgruppe) did make Lion briefly ; the bulk of Pz-Rgt 22 was repulsed at Bieville and never closed the coastal gap.

events.title: Arromanches-les-Bains taken (1 Dorsets)
events.description: 1 Dorsets (231 Brigade) take Arromanches-les-Bains, securing the future Mulberry B harbour site.

events.title: Colleville-sur-Mer contested (1st ID)
events.description: 16th RCT pushes into Colleville-sur-Mer; village changes hands at least once before nightfall.

events.title: Lebisey ridge: 3rd ID stopped short of Caen
events.description: British 3rd Division line stops at the Lebisey ridge ~6 km short of Caen, blocked by 21. Panzer's defensive posture. The objective Montgomery had set for D-Day will not fall for another month.

disputedBy.claim: Whether Caen was a realistic D-Day objective for 3rd ID is one of the most-disputed questions of the campaign. Harrison concludes the schedule was unrealistic given depth of advance and 21. Panzer's counterattack ; Beevor (cross-check) pushes back, noting at least 7 hours' delay before 9 Brigade was committed inland.

events.title: 50th Northumbrian approaches Bayeux
events.description: Lead 50th Division elements (56 Brigade recce) reach the outskirts of Bayeux but do not enter the city today (Bayeux liberated 7 June).

events.title: End of MVP window: tenuous beachhead established
events.description: By 18:00, all five beaches and both US airborne areas have been landed and partially consolidated. Omaha remains the most precarious; the airborne are scattered but holding key inland anchors. None of the planned D-Day objectives (Bayeux, Caen) have been taken yet.
